newsSinceJun6: No material company-specific news since June 6. Latest PR is June 1 (PCIM Expo 2026 showcase, June 9-11 Nuremberg: AI GPU/SoC multiphase controllers AOZ73216QI/AOZ73104QI, 240W Type-C EPR switches, 48V-800V MOSFET/SiC/GaN). Today's -9.98% move has no identifiable fundamental catalyst - it reads as flow-driven profit-taking/momentum unwind after a ~161% 90-day run (sell-the-news into the PCIM start). The May fiscal-Q3 EPS miss and analyst PT cuts are stale, pre-June-6 information.

sources.label: StockTitan AOSL news feed (latest items June 1 and May 18; nothing June 6-9)
BusinessWire - AOS at PCIM Expo 2026 (June 1 PR)
Investing.com - fiscal Q3 2026 earnings transcript (EPS miss, May; stale context for the prior de-rate)
Simply Wall St - AOSL AI power push / 90-day +161% momentum backdrop

verdicts.rankRationale: Yesterday's down-move was explicitly crowding-driven (less hidden after +113.9% YTD); today's flow-driven -10% with zero new negative fundamentals partially restores the hiddenness this page penalized, so the crowding lens flips to a modest up. Thesis (selective AI/edge power exposure) is unchanged.
verdicts.bull: AI-core-power product cadence is real (PCIM lineup, Intel Panther Lake total power solution), and a 10% one-day wash-out with no fundamental news improves entry asymmetry on a $1.5B name.
verdicts.neutral: Still +92.6% YTD and only indirectly levered to the true CPO/HBM/packaging bottlenecks; one red day does not make it hidden again.
verdicts.bear: May quarter showed an EPS miss with legacy segments shrinking; if the momentum crowd keeps unwinding, a high-beta $1.5B power name can give back far more than 10% before fundamentals matter.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: PCIM 2026 (June 9-11) design-win or AI data-center power announcements converting product showcase into bookings.
verdicts.primaryRisk: Continued momentum unwind in crowded small-cap AI-power names plus legacy-segment cyclical drag.

verdicts.rankRationale: Power-semis watchlist fit is unchanged and there is no new fundamental evidence since June 6; the -10% day is flow, not thesis, so hold per yesterday's verdict with a small positive drift for improved asymmetry (YTD now 92.6% vs 113.9%).
verdicts.bull: 48V-800V power-conversion portfolio (MOSFET/SiC/GaN plus multiphase controllers) sits squarely on the data-center power-density scaling curve, and today's de-rate cheapens the exposure.
verdicts.neutral: No new contracts, capacity, or guidance since June 6; the scaling thesis neither strengthened nor weakened - only the price changed.
verdicts.bear: Sub-scale ($1.5B) versus MPWR/Infineon/onsemi in AI power; if AI power-tree sockets consolidate with the leaders, AOSL's scaling exposure stays optional rather than bottleneck-critical.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: Concrete AI data-center power design wins (GPU VR sockets, 48V/800V architectures) announced at or after PCIM 2026.
verdicts.primaryRisk: Execution/share-loss against larger power-management incumbents while legacy compute/consumer segments keep declining.
